description: Per-bucket placement policy + cross-cluster rebalance worker. Closes ROADMAP P2 line 185. Adds meta.Bucket.Placement={cluster:weight} policy, chunk PUT routes via stable fnv32a hash-mod, new `strata server --workers=rebalance` leader-elected mover (RADOS + S3 sides) copies chunks A->B until actual distribution matches the policy, plus safety rails (refuse >90% target fill, drain sentinel via cluster_state row). Operator workflow: register new cluster -> set Placement -> drain old -> rebalance worker converges -> deregister via env. Zero behavior change for existing buckets without a policy (Placement=nil short-circuits to $defaultCluster).

userStories.title: meta.Bucket.Placement field + admin API + contract test
userStories.description: As an operator, I want to set and inspect a placement policy on a bucket so I can control how its chunks spread across clusters.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `Placement map[string]int` field to `meta.Bucket` with `json:"placement,omitempty"` tag

Add `meta.Store` triple `SetBucketPlacement(ctx, name, map[string]int) error`, `GetBucketPlacement(ctx, name) (map[string]int, error)`, `DeleteBucketPlacement(ctx, name) error` and implement on memory + cassandra (via blob-config helper `setBucketBlob`/`getBucketBlob`/`deleteBucketBlob`) + tikv backends

Validation: weight `[0, 100]`, `sum(weights) > 0` else `ErrInvalidPlacement`, cluster name must resolve to a live entry in `STRATA_RADOS_CLUSTERS` / `STRATA_S3_CLUSTERS` else `ErrUnknownCluster` (both new sentinel errors in `meta/store.go`)
Get on a bucket without policy returns `(nil, nil)` — NOT an error — so the routing path can fall back to `$defaultCluster`
Admin API endpoints `PUT/GET/DELETE /admin/v1/buckets/{name}/placement` wired in `internal/adminapi/`; GET returns 404 when unset
OpenAPI spec at `internal/adminapi/openapi.yaml` updated to describe the new placement endpoints + request/response schemas
Audit row stamped via `s3api.SetAuditOverride(ctx, "admin:PutBucketPlacement", ...)` on PUT/DELETE
Contract test `caseBucketPlacement` added to `internal/meta/storetest/contract.go` exercising all three backends (round-trip + nil-policy fallback + validation failures)
Unit tests on the validation path (weight out-of-range, sum=0, unknown cluster)
`go vet ./...` passes
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: PutChunks consults policy with hash-mod stable routing
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the chunk PUT path to route via the placement policy so chunks spread per the operator's weights and the same chunk always lands on the same cluster across retries.
acceptanceCriteria: New helper `internal/data/placement/router.go::PickCluster(bucketID uuid.UUID, key string, chunkIdx int, policy map[string]int) string` using `fnv32a("<bucketID>/<key>/<chunkIdx>") % sum(weights)` walking the weight wheel over a sorted-cluster-id slice
Empty/nil policy → returns `""` so caller falls back to existing `$defaultCluster` behavior (no breaking change for unconfigured buckets)
Zero-weight clusters in the policy are never picked
Deterministic: 1000 random `(key, idx)` pairs produce identical output across repeat calls (unit test)
Distribution: 10000 chunks with weights `{a:1, b:1}` split ~50/50 (within 5%); weights `{a:1, b:3}` split ~25/75 (within 5%) (unit tests)

`internal/data/rados/Backend.PutChunks` reads `Bucket.Placement` (passed via the existing call path that already has the bucket struct) and routes each chunk via `placement.PickCluster`; falls back to `$defaultCluster` when picker returns `""`

`internal/data/s3/Backend.PutChunks` same pattern — calls `placement.PickCluster` and uses the returned cluster to resolve the per-cluster `*awss3.Client` + bucket via existing `resolveClass` plumbing
Backwards-compat unit test: buckets with `Placement == nil` route exactly as today (regression-test against existing single-cluster RADOS / S3 fixtures)
`go vet ./...` passes
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Rebalance worker scaffold + leader election + plan emission
userStories.description: As an operator, I want a background worker that periodically scans buckets and reports actual-vs-target chunk distribution so I can see migration progress without code-diving.
acceptanceCriteria: New file `cmd/strata/workers/rebalance.go` registers a worker named `rebalance` via `init()` calling `workers.Register(workers.Worker{Name: "rebalance", Build: ..., SkipLease: false})`
New package `internal/rebalance/` with `Worker` implementing `workers.Runner`
Leader-elected on outer `rebalance-leader` lease (`SkipLease: false` — single leader cluster-wide)

Envs read at constructor time via `os.Getenv`: `STRATA_REBALANCE_INTERVAL` (Go duration, default `1h`, range `[1m, 24h]`), `STRATA_REBALANCE_RATE_MB_S` (int, default `100`, range `[1, 10000]`), `STRATA_REBALANCE_INFLIGHT` (int, default `4`, range `[1, 64]`); out-of-range values clamped + WARN-logged

Per-iteration: walk `meta.Store.ListAllBuckets`; for each bucket with non-nil `Placement` call `scanDistribution(bucket)` that returns `map[cluster]chunkCount` (actual) + `[]Move{ObjectKey, ChunkIdx, FromCluster, ToCluster}`

`executeMoves(plan)` is a stub returning `nil` in this story (mover wired in US-004/US-005); worker logs INFO `"rebalance plan"` with `bucket=<name>`, `moves=<n>`, `actual=<map>`, `target=<map>` per bucket
Iteration span: `tracer.Start(ctx, "worker.rebalance.tick")` via the shared iteration_span helper from `internal/otel`; sub-op span `rebalance.scan_bucket` per bucket scan
Per-iteration counter metric `strata_rebalance_planned_moves_total{bucket}` incremented per planned move
Worker registered in supervisor list; `STRATA_WORKERS=rebalance` accepted by `workers.Resolve`
Unit tests cover the scan loop against an in-memory `meta.Store` with seeded `Bucket{Placement}` + a fake manifest reader; no real data backend required
`go vet ./...` passes
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: RADOS-side mover — Read + Write + manifest CAS + GC enqueue
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the rebalance worker to actually move chunks between RADOS clusters so the distribution converges on the policy without manual `rados cp` ceremony.
acceptanceCriteria: New file `internal/rebalance/rados_mover.go` exposing `Move(ctx, src, tgt RadosCluster, plan []Move) error` (interface-typed `RadosCluster` so unit tests can fake without librados)
Per move: `Read(srcIoctx, oid)` → `Write(tgtIoctx, newOID)` (new random OID to avoid cross-pool name collisions); collect into per-object batches keyed `(bucketID, objectKey)`

Per-object batched manifest CAS via existing `meta.Store.SetObjectStorage(ctx, bucket, key, versionID, expectedClass, newClass, updatedManifest)` — single LWT per object even when multiple chunks moved (pass `expectedClass == currentClass` because rebalance doesn't change class)
On manifest CAS success: enqueue OLD `{Pool, OID}` into the GC queue via existing `meta.Store.EnqueueGCEntry`

On manifest CAS reject (`applied=false`): enqueue the NEW (target) `{Pool, OID}` into GC instead so the unused copy gets cleaned, increment `strata_rebalance_cas_conflicts_total{bucket}` counter, and continue with the next object
Token-bucket bandwidth throttle from `golang.org/x/time/rate` at `STRATA_REBALANCE_RATE_MB_S` (bytes/sec); both read + write consume from the same bucket so `chunkSize × 2` tokens per move
Bounded `errgroup` worker pool at `STRATA_REBALANCE_INFLIGHT`
Metrics: `strata_rebalance_bytes_moved_total{from,to}` (counter), `strata_rebalance_chunks_moved_total{from,to,bucket}` (counter)
Per-move span `rebalance.move_chunk` with attrs `bucket`, `object_key`, `from`, `to`, `chunk_idx`

Two-cluster integration test (gated by the `integration` build tag; gracefully `t.Skip` when only one RADOS cluster is available in the test env): plant 100 chunks via PUT against cluster `c1`, flip Placement to `{c1:0, c2:1}`, run one rebalance tick, assert (a) 100 chunks now visible on `c2`, (b) old OIDs enqueued in GC queue, (c) manifests reference the new pool
`go vet ./...` passes
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: S3-side mover — CopyObject + Get/Put fallback
userStories.description: As an operator running multi-cluster S3-over-S3, I want the rebalance worker to move chunks between S3 clusters using the cheapest available SDK path.
acceptanceCriteria: New file `internal/rebalance/s3_mover.go` exposing `Move(ctx, src, tgt S3Cluster, plan []Move) error` (interface-typed `S3Cluster` so unit tests can fake without minio)
If `src.Endpoint == tgt.Endpoint && src.Region == tgt.Region` → `awss3.CopyObject` (server-side copy — no bytes through gateway)
Else → `GetObject` from src streaming into `PutObject` on tgt via `manager.Uploader` (chunk-size buffered, no full-object materialisation)
Per-object batched manifest CAS via `meta.Store.SetObjectStorage` — same shape as RADOS mover
On CAS success: enqueue OLD `{Pool, OID}` into GC queue; on CAS reject: enqueue NEW (target) `{Pool, OID}` into GC and increment `strata_rebalance_cas_conflicts_total{bucket}`
Throttle + concurrency primitives shared with RADOS mover via `internal/rebalance/throttle.go` (token bucket + bounded errgroup helpers)
Metrics: same `_bytes_moved_total` + `_chunks_moved_total` counters (labels `from`/`to` carry S3 cluster ID)
Per-move span `rebalance.move_chunk` (same shape as RADOS mover)
Integration test with two minio testcontainers (tagged `integration`): plant 100 chunks on `c1`, set Placement `{c1:0, c2:1}`, run rebalance, assert chunks on `c2` and GC queue contains the old keys
`go vet ./...` passes
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Safety rails — cluster fill probe + drain sentinel + admin API
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the rebalance worker to refuse moves when the target is nearly full or when the source is mid-drain so I don't trigger a cascade or fight an in-flight deregistration.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `data.Backend.ClusterStats(ctx, clusterID string) (usedBytes, totalBytes int64, err error)` interface method; RADOS implementation calls `(*Conn).MonCommand({"prefix":"df"})` parsed JSON; S3 implementation returns `ErrNotSupported`

Rebalance worker refuses to dispatch a move when `target.used/target.total > 0.90` (RADOS-only — S3 `ErrNotSupported` is treated as 'OK to proceed' with a one-line WARN per iteration); refusal logs WARN `"rebalance refused: target full"` with `bucket`, `target` labels; increments `strata_rebalance_refused_total{reason="target_full",target}`

New `cluster_state` row keyed on `clusterID` storing `state ∈ {"live", "draining", "removed"}` via blob-config helpers in `meta.Store` (memory + cassandra + tikv); default is `"live"` (absence of row == live, no row required for the common case)

Admin API `POST /admin/v1/clusters/{id}/drain` flips state to `"draining"`, `POST /admin/v1/clusters/{id}/undrain` flips back to `"live"`, `GET /admin/v1/clusters` lists every cluster from env with state + last-scan chunk-count snapshot
OpenAPI spec at `internal/adminapi/openapi.yaml` updated to describe the cluster-state endpoints
Rebalance worker refuses moves INTO a `draining` cluster — same metric with label `reason="target_draining"`
Rebalance worker ALLOWS moves OUT of a `draining` cluster — that's the whole point of the operator workflow

`placement.PickCluster` extended with a `drainingClusters map[string]bool` arg; entries in this set are skipped (treated as weight=0); if all clusters in the policy are draining → returns `""` so caller falls back to `$defaultCluster`
In-process cache for the drain-state lookup with 30s TTL so the PUT hot path isn't burdened (`internal/rebalance/draincache.go` or similar)
Audit row stamped on drain/undrain admin API calls

Integration test: register 2 clusters, drain `c1`, verify (a) `GET /admin/v1/clusters` shows `c1.state=draining`, (b) new PUTs route to `c2` even when Placement is `{c1:1, c2:0}`, (c) rebalance worker moves chunks `c1 → c2`, (d) attempting to rebalance INTO a `c2` simulated as 95% full is refused
`go vet ./...` passes
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Docs + ROADMAP close-flip + PRD removal
userStories.description: As a future operator, I need the operator workflow + tuning knobs documented so I can run a real cluster drain without code-diving.
acceptanceCriteria: New page `docs/site/content/best-practices/placement-rebalance.md` covering the operator workflow (register → set Placement → drain → rebalance → deregister), `STRATA_REBALANCE_*` env tuning table, troubleshooting (CAS conflict storms, target-full refusals, drain-cache TTL)
`docs/site/content/best-practices/_index.md` gets a link entry for the new page
`docs/site/content/architecture/observability.md` adds the `strata_rebalance_*` metric family + `worker.rebalance.tick` / `rebalance.scan_bucket` / `rebalance.move_chunk` span shapes

Project root `CLAUDE.md` 'Background workers' section gets a new bullet describing `strata server --workers=rebalance` (env names, leader lease `rebalance-leader`, what meta + data surfaces it touches, note that `placement.PickCluster` consults the `cluster_state` drain map)

`ROADMAP.md` line 185 (the P2 entry) close-flip to `~~**P2 — Per-bucket placement policy + cross-cluster rebalance worker.**~~ — **Done.** <one-line summary referencing meta.Bucket.Placement, PickCluster routing, rebalance worker with RADOS+S3 movers, drain sentinel, and safety rails>. (commit `<pending>`)` — closing SHA backfilled in a follow-up commit on main per CLAUDE.md convention
`tasks/prd-placement-rebalance.md` REMOVED in the same commit (PRD lifecycle rule — Ralph snapshot under `scripts/ralph/archive/` is canonical)
`make docs-build` succeeds
`make vet` succeeds
`make test` succeeds
